key_metrics_output: Polymer type (PE, PP, PS, PVC, PMMA, PC), mass concentration, analytical recovery (78.7–90.1%), quantification limits (0.014–0.29 µg)
abstract: A pyrolysis-gas chromatography-mass spectrometry protocol was developed for monitoring six common polymers (polyethylene, polypropylene, polystyrene, polyvinyl chloride, polymethyl methacrylate, and polycarbonate) in environmental samples. Polymer-specific solvent systems were used for calibration. Testing on spiked soil matrices demonstrated analytical recovery of 78.7–90.1% with polymer quantification limits ranging from 0.014 to 0.29 µg.

key_metrics_output: polymer quantification limits ranging from 0.014 µg to 0.29 µg
Results on spiked soil matrices showed 78.7–90.1% analytical recovery
monitoring of polyethylene, polypropylene, polystyrene, polyvinyl chloride, polymethyl methacrylate, and polycarbonate
abstract: The ubiquitous occurrence of microplastics in ecosystems and living organisms is an emerging issue of health concern, yet robust risk assessment is limited by the absence of standardized analytical methods.

We developed a pyrolysis-gas chromatography-mass spectrometry protocol for the monitoring of polyethylene, polypropylene, polystyrene, polyvinyl chloride, polymethyl methacrylate, and polycarbonate in environmental samples.
Results on spiked soil matrices showed 78.7–90.1% analytical recovery and polymer quantification limits ranging from 0.014 µg to 0.29 µg.
